description: Create test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol covering FR-15 and FR-16 using the existing mocks in test/mocks/AdversarialTokens.sol and test/mocks/ReentrantReceiver.sol. Verifies balance-diff accounting correctly handles fee-on-transfer (1% burn), rebasing up/down, no-return (USDT-style) and false-returning tokens, and that nonReentrant on the Router swap entry point blocks re-entry via a callback token.
commit: test(router): add adversarial-token and reentrancy tests
steps: Create test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol sharing the RouterTestBase deployment. Extend ReentrantReceiver to target Router instead of ExecutionProxy (add Router setup functions; or write a RouterReentrantReceiver in test/mocks/).

Fee-on-transfer tests: test_FOT_InputToken (FeeOnTransferToken as inputToken with 1% burn; Router.balanceOf(input) ≈ pulled==inputAmount*99/100; protocol + partner fee computed off pulled; executor receives pulled-fees; final user amount >= outputMin or tx reverts SlippageExceeded); test_FOT_OutputToken (FOT as outputToken; swap succeeds when outputMin set below the 1%-burn level, reverts when outputMin too tight).

Rebasing tests: test_Rebasing_OutputPositiveRebase (RebasingToken as outputToken; MockDEX is programmed to mint RebasingToken and the test invokes RebasingToken.rebaseUp(500) *between* outputBefore snapshot and outputAfter snapshot -- specifically via a Weiroll command sequence that calls rebaseUp inside executePath -- so Router's balance-diff measures the post-rebase amount; positive-slippage cap still applies); test_Rebasing_OutputNegativeRebase (rebaseDown(500) during executor call causes balance-diff amountOut < outputMin and Router reverts SlippageExceeded).

Weird-ERC20 tests: test_NoReturnToken_Input (USDT-style token with no return value works via SafeERC20); test_FalseReturningToken_Input_Reverts (SafeERC20 catches false return and bubbles); test_FalseReturningToken_Output (swap outputMin still enforced after SafeERC20 transfer -- SafeERC20 reverts on false return on the final _transferOut to the recipient).

Reentrancy tests: test_Reentrancy_ViaCallbackToken (CallbackToken with callback enabled on Router address; receiver is a RouterReentrantReceiver that re-enters swap() inside onTokenTransfer; outer tx reverts with ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall or the inner try/catch records attackSucceeded==false while outer swap completes and Router cannot be drained); test_Reentrancy_ViaETHReceive (receiver is contract that re-enters swap on receive; same guard behavior).
Run `forge test --match-path test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol` and confirm all pass.
acceptance_criteria: [auto] File test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol exists; `forge test --match-path test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol` passes.
[auto] grep -n 'FeeOnTransferToken\|RebasingToken\|NoReturnToken\|FalseReturningToken\|CallbackToken' test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol returns >= 5 matches (all five mocks exercised).
[auto] grep -n 'ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall' test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol returns at least one match (the OZ custom error selector).
[auto] All tests pass under `forge test --match-path test/Router.Adversarial.t.sol -v` with zero failures.

[manual] Reentrancy test wraps the inner swap call in try/catch, asserts catch branch recorded the ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall selector, and asserts after the outer tx that router.balanceOf(inputToken) and router.balanceOf(outputToken) are exactly the values expected by the non-reentrant accounting (no inflation from the attempted re-entry).
